title: Fixing Broken HTML Document Structure with Missing Head Tags

statement.markdown: Given a string representing the content of an HTML document, your task is to validate that the document has a correctly structured head section. A head section is considered valid if it contains a title tag and at least one of the following tags: meta, link, or script. If the head section is valid, return 'Valid'. Otherwise, return 'Invalid'.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be a string containing the HTML document content, possibly with HTML tags.
statement.outputFormat: The function should return a string indicating whether the head section is valid.
statement.notes: The function should handle cases where the head section is missing, malformed, or incomplete.

constraints.description: The length of the input string should not exceed 10,000 characters.

examples.explanation: The head section contains a title tag and a meta tag.
The head section is missing a meta, link, or script tag.
The head section is missing a title tag.

starterCode.python:
class Solution:
